You're absorbing the statistics. You're filtering what's relevant. You're taking notes and you're trying to observe and interpret body language. When you see people who are able to answer questions on the spot while under pressure, it's not because they're smarter than you. They don't just rely on their memory. They fall back onto frameworks. They are the scaffolding that holds you steady when the heat is on. And one of the most robust frameworks you should master in a work setting, it's called PREP, P-R-E-P. And the PREP framework, the P stands for point. Start with one main idea first. The R stands for reason. Give one reason why your point matters. The E stands for example, support it with either data, a story, or a concrete illustration. And then the P, you're reiterating the point. Restate the point. Circle back and reinforce your main idea.
